hero.title: La Bergerie & La Brassinne — two cocoons to reconnect
hero.description: Two charming gites designed for a stay for two in Libin, in the heart of the Belgian Ardennes. La Bergerie offers more space and light; La Brassinne is suitable for guests with reduced mobility and all on one level.
hero.imageAlt: View of the cottage property in Libin

stats.label: gites designed for a stay for two
in the heart of the Belgian Ardennes

intro.title: A thoughtful welcome, almost like home
paragraphs: La Bergerie & La Brassinne are two charming cottages tucked away in Libin, in the Ardennes. They were designed inside an authentic old building, with soft materials, quality comforts and details that instantly set the tone.
Beds are made before you arrive, towels are ready, the kitchen is truly equipped, and the welcome stays personal and genuine throughout the experience.
Before arrival, guests can easily compare both cottages, find the useful practical details and choose the simplest way to book.

highlights.title: Designed for stays for two
highlights.text: Each gite welcomes 2 guests in a romantic, cocooning and reassuring atmosphere. Children are accepted and pets may be possible on request.

highlights.title: A region perfect for weekends
highlights.text: Walks, restaurants, charming villages and memorable discoveries around Libin, Redu, Mirwart and Saint-Hubert.

highlights.title: Simple direct booking
highlights.text: WhatsApp, e-mail, form or Airbnb depending on what feels easiest for the traveller.

highlights.title: Romantic extras
highlights.text: Champagne, flowers, romantic decoration, gift vouchers or curated surprise boxes can all fit naturally into the stay.

gites.title: Two moods, the same sense of charm
gites.description: La Bergerie stands out for its space and light. La Brassinne is smaller, cosier and more intimate. Both share the same attention to detail and warm hospitality.

bergerie.name: La Bergerie
bergerie.description: The larger of the two gites, ideal if you like a soft shabby chic decor, a garden at the back and a small terrace at the front.
bullets: Back garden and small front terrace

brassine.name: La Brassinne
brassine.description: Smaller, cosier and designed for privacy. A reduced-mobility suitable retreat, all on one level, with a garden at the front and a garden at the back.

features.title: Everything guests need, without the usual friction
features.description: Everything is prepared to make arrival smooth, the stay comfortable and each exchange simple through to check-out.

items.title: Beds ready on arrival
items.text: Quality linen and an instantly comfortable feeling from the very first minutes.

items.title: A truly equipped kitchen
items.text: Oven, dishwasher, coffee machine, wine glasses and all the essentials for cooking together.

items.title: Wi-Fi throughout
items.text: Stable connection in the whole property, with Ethernet available if needed.

items.title: 4K TV and streaming
items.text: Perfect for cosy evenings, rainy afternoons or slow moments after a walk.

items.title: Easy parking
items.text: 7 parking spaces in total, paid electric charging available and an in-person welcome.

items.title: Warm welcome
items.text: In-person key handover, local advice and a host who stays available if needed.

romantic.title: Plan a romantic surprise
romantic.description: Champagne, a box, romantic decoration or a gift voucher can be discussed on request, depending on your wishes and what can be prepared.

items.title: Romantic decoration

gallery.title: Browse the atmosphere like a keepsake album

meta.title: La Bergerie - Romantic cottage in Libin
meta.description: The larger of the two charming cottages in Libin. Romantic, bright, with a rear garden, equipped kitchen and refined shabby chic atmosphere.

hero.title: La Bergerie
hero.description: A more spacious, luminous and gentle escape in a soft shabby chic setting designed for two.

story.title: A gentle place filled with light
story.lead: La Bergerie is the larger of the two cottages. Guests love its comfortable proportions, natural light and feeling of space without losing the intimacy of a romantic stay.
paragraphs: Inside this old Ardennes building, the decor blends natural materials, soft tones and refined shabby chic details that give the stay a real sense of personality.
The living area invites you to slow down, the bedroom feels restful and the kitchen makes it easy to enjoy a dinner for two without compromise.
At the back, the garden extends the experience with a peaceful outdoor corner for coffee, aperitif or a quiet moment.

bergerie.galleryIntro: Discover La Bergerie in pictures: light, soft textures and a romantic atmosphere.
highlights: The most spacious of the two cottages
Beds ready on arrival and quality linen
Ideal for a peaceful romantic weekend
Children accepted and pets on request

stayInfo.title: Arrival & welcome
stayInfo.text: Check-in is between 4 pm and 9 pm, with in-person key handover and a warm personal welcome.

stayInfo.title: Daily comfort
stayInfo.text: Towels provided, beds prepared, Wi-Fi, television, full kitchen and an available host if needed.

stayInfo.title: Parking
stayInfo.text: Free parking on site. An electric charging point is available, and use of it is paid.

reviewHighlight.author: Highlighted review
reviewHighlight.text: A stay that combines charm, comfort and a genuine feeling of being well looked after. La Bergerie often appeals to couples who want more space and a peaceful mood.

outdoor.title: The rear garden and small front terrace
outdoor.text: La Bergerie has a garden at the back and a small terrace at the front, so you can enjoy quiet outdoor moments throughout the day.

bergerie.faqIntro: A cottage-specific FAQ to help guests picture the stay and reduce repetitive questions before booking or arrival.

faq.question: Is La Bergerie larger than La Brassinne?
faq.answer: Yes. It is the more spacious cottage, which many couples appreciate when they want a little more room to enjoy the stay.

faq.question: Is the garden easy to access?
faq.answer: Yes, La Bergerie opens onto the rear garden, which is one of its strongest assets.

faq.question: Can we cook properly on site?
faq.answer: Yes. The kitchen is well equipped with oven, stove, dishwasher, coffee machine and the essentials for cooking together.

faq.question: Is it suitable for a special occasion?
faq.answer: Absolutely. It works beautifully for a romantic surprise, anniversary or a carefully prepared couple's weekend.

faq.question: Are children and pets accepted?
faq.answer: The gites are designed first and foremost for a stay for two, but children are accepted. Pets may be accepted on request before booking.

faq.question: Are cleaning fees included?
faq.answer: Yes, cleaning fees are included.

faq.question: Is there a barbecue?
faq.answer: Yes, a barbecue is available. Conditions of use may depend on the season and the weather.

meta.title: La Brassinne - Cosy romantic cottage in Libin
meta.description: A smaller, more intimate and cocooning gite in Libin, suitable for guests with reduced mobility, all on one level, with a garden at the front and garden at the back.

hero.title: La Brassinne
hero.description: A deeply cosy retreat, more intimate and more enveloping, ideal for a couple's break, a restful moment or a quiet stay.

story.title: A little nest designed for intimacy
story.lead: La Brassinne is smaller than La Bergerie, and that is exactly part of its charm. It feels like a warm retreat, easy to live in and especially pleasant for two.
paragraphs: The cottage is suitable for guests with reduced mobility and entirely on one level, which makes the stay simple and comfortable. The atmosphere is romantic, soft and cocooning from the very first steps inside.
Each space has been designed to create immediate well-being: a cosy bedroom, a welcoming living space and everything needed to cook, unwind and enjoy being together.
The front garden reinforces that feeling of privacy, and the garden at the back also offers a more open outdoor space.

brassine.galleryIntro: Discover La Brassinne in pictures: softness, privacy and charm.
highlights: The most cocooning of the two cottages
Suitable for guests with reduced mobility and all on one level
Garden at the front and garden at the back
Children accepted and pets on request

stayInfo.title: Arrival & welcome
stayInfo.text: Check-in is between 4 pm and 9 pm, with in-person key handover and a caring welcome.

stayInfo.title: Cosy comfort
stayInfo.text: Beds made, towels ready, Wi-Fi and all the essentials for a stay you can fully enjoy indoors as well.

stayInfo.title: Accessibility & outdoors
stayInfo.text: La Brassinne is suitable for guests with reduced mobility and all on one level. It has a front garden and access to a garden at the back.

reviewHighlight.author: Highlighted review
reviewHighlight.text: La Brassinne is often chosen for its deeply intimate atmosphere. Guests love the cocooning feeling and the sense of a true couple's hideaway.

outdoor.title: The front and back gardens
outdoor.text: La Brassinne has a garden at the front and a garden at the back, extending the cosy atmosphere with two gentle outdoor spaces.

brassine.faqIntro: A dedicated FAQ to help guests immediately understand the mood and experience of La Brassinne.

faq.question: Is La Brassinne smaller than La Bergerie?
faq.answer: Yes, it is more compact, which is exactly what makes it feel especially cosy and intimate.

faq.question: Is La Brassinne suitable for guests with reduced mobility?
faq.answer: Yes, La Brassinne is suitable for guests with reduced mobility and is all on one level. For any specific accessibility question, please contact us before your stay so we can confirm that the accommodation matches your needs.

faq.question: Does it feel different from La Bergerie?
faq.answer: Yes. La Brassinne is more enveloping and intimate, with a true cocooning atmosphere for couples.

faq.question: Can we still enjoy the whole stay on site?
faq.answer: Absolutely. The kitchen is equipped, the TV allows streaming and everything supports a comfortable slow stay indoors.

faq.question: Are children and pets accepted?
faq.answer: The gites are designed first and foremost for a stay for two, but children are accepted. Pets may be accepted on request before booking.

faq.question: Are cleaning fees included?
faq.answer: Yes, cleaning fees are included.

faq.question: Is there a barbecue?
faq.answer: Yes, a barbecue is available. Conditions of use may depend on the season and the weather.

meta.title: Experiences & surroundings - Libin and the Ardennes
meta.description: Restaurants, walks, romantic weekend ideas, charming villages and activities around Libin, Redu, Mirwart and Saint-Hubert.

hero.title: The Ardennes the way we love to recommend them
hero.description: Not just a list of places, but a curated selection that fits the spirit of the stay: beautiful, gentle, local and memorable.

intro.title: Lovely ideas around Libin
intro.text: This structure is easy to enrich later with your own addresses. It already includes coherent local recommendations to guide guests before and during the stay.

sections.title: Restaurants
sections.intro: Five easy-to-recommend places for a good meal during the stay, with Les Frangins first for staying close to the gites.

items.name: Les Frangins
items.description: A family brasserie-grill in Libin, ideal for a meal close to the gites in a warm setting.

items.name: Le Moulin de la Dîme
items.description: A warm restaurant in Redu, centred on local produce and the atmosphere of the mill.

items.name: Le Cor de Chasse
items.description: A refined option when you want to make the evening feel more special.

items.name: Le Relais de Mirwart
items.description: The setting, the village and the nature around it make this a lovely outing for two.

sections.title: Walks
sections.intro: Easy-to-recommend walks depending on mood: forest, villages, water, viewpoints.

items.name: Mirwart estate
items.description: A classic Ardennes walk with heritage, forest atmosphere and beautiful scenery.

items.name: Around Redu
items.description: Ideal for combining a gentle walk with bookshops, galleries and a coffee stop.

items.name: Saint-Hubert nature side
items.description: A good option for travellers who want both heritage and woodland surroundings.

items.name: Balade du Bourgmestre
items.description: A marked loop starting at Libin church, perfect for discovering the forests and plateaus around the village.

items.name: Lesse walk
items.description: A nature walk along the Lesse, between forest, river and wilder landscapes.

sections.title: Romantic weekend ideas
sections.intro: A few easy combinations that turn two days into a truly memorable break.

items.name: Arrive and slow down
items.description: A drink, a hot shower, a simple dinner in the cottage and a cosy evening indoors.

items.name: Walk then dinner
items.description: A walk in Mirwart or Redu followed by a beautiful dinner together.

items.name: Surprise your partner
items.description: Add champagne, flowers or romantic decoration to make the stay even more memorable.

items.name: No heavy schedule
items.description: A few landmarks, very little pressure and plenty of time to simply enjoy being together.

sections.title: Sunny-day activities
sections.intro: For guests who like adding a few carefully chosen outings to their stay.

items.name: Redu book village
items.description: A very pleasant outing among bookshops, workshops and a unique atmosphere.

items.name: Euro Space Center
items.description: An original and easy excursion if you want something different.

items.name: Cycling rides
items.description: Roads and scenery work beautifully for a relaxed or more active ride.

items.name: Saint-Hubert heritage
items.description: A nice option to combine architecture, atmosphere and a terrace stop.

sections.title: Rainy-day activities
sections.intro: A great stay does not depend on sunshine. Rain works surprisingly well in the Ardennes.

items.name: Cosy day in the cottage
items.description: Streaming, board games, cooking together and the pleasure of staying warm inside.

items.name: Redu bookshops and galleries
items.description: The book village remains charming even under grey skies.

items.name: A long lunch
items.description: Use the rain as the perfect excuse for a slower, more indulgent meal.

items.name: Local heritage
items.description: Museums, basilica or village visits depending on the mood of the day.

sections.title: Villages & heritage
sections.intro: Easy local references for guests who want to explore without driving too far.

items.name: Libin
items.description: An ideal base to explore the region while keeping travel times light.

items.name: Redu
items.description: The famous book village remains one of the area's most charming outings.

items.name: Saint-Hubert
items.description: A town with character, easy to recommend for its heritage and atmosphere.

items.name: Mirwart
items.description: A peaceful and photogenic village, very much in tune with the spirit of the stay.

items.name: Lesse
items.description: A bucolic hamlet by the Lesse, perfect for a nature walk around Redu.

sections.title: Bike rides
sections.intro: A few lovely rides to discover the area at an easy pace or on a more active outing.

items.name: Gentle loop around Libin
items.description: For couples who want an easy ride without planning a full sports day.

items.name: Towards Redu and nearby villages
items.description: A lovely route combining countryside, charm and a gourmet stop.

items.name: More sportive Ardennes version
items.description: A more demanding ride for guests who enjoy elevation and longer loops in the Ardennes.

finalCta.title: Need help planning the stay?
finalCta.text: The practical guide answers the most common questions, and the booking page lets guests choose WhatsApp, the form or Airbnb.

meta.title: Practical guide - Prepare your stay in Libin
meta.description: Everything guests need before arrival: check-in, check-out, parking, linen, Wi-Fi, television, heating, waste sorting and departure.

hero.title: Useful, simple and reassuring information
hero.description: Find the useful details to arrive calmly, enjoy the cottage and prepare check-out without stress.

sections.title: Before you arrive
sections.text: A few simple details help make the stay feel effortless: confirm your timing, plan your route, check what to bring and let us know about any special request.

points.title: Before-arrival checklist
points.text: Confirm your arrival time, prepare your route, share any special request and contact us if you have an accessibility question.

points.title: Already prepared
points.text: Linen is provided, beds are prepared, Wi-Fi is available and parking is available on site.

sections.title: Arrival & check-in
sections.text: Check-in takes place between 4 pm and 9 pm with an in-person, human welcome. We agree on your arrival time with you so we can welcome you in the best conditions.

points.title: On-site welcome
points.text: Keys are handed over in person in a simple, warm and reassuring way.

points.title: Directions
points.text: The address is Rue du Curé 19, 6890 Libin. The directions button in the guide opens Google Maps.

sections.title: During the stay
sections.text: Everything is there so guests can enjoy the cottage without improvising. The kitchen is properly equipped, Wi-Fi is available throughout and the TV supports streaming.

points.title: Kitchen
points.text: Oven, stove, microwave, dishwasher, fridge, freezer, coffee machine, kettle, toaster, dishes, wine glasses and cooking essentials., juicer.

points.title: TV & streaming
points.text: Sign in to your own streaming service if needed, and please remember to sign out before leaving.

points.title: Wi-Fi
points.text: Wi-Fi covers the whole property. Precise connection details are shared on site.

points.title: EV charging
points.text: An electric vehicle charging point is available. Use is paid, so it is best to mention it before arrival.

points.title: Heat pump
points.text: The system is already set for comfortable temperatures. If something feels unusual, it is better to contact the host rather than changing multiple settings.

points.title: Waste sorting
points.text: Guests are asked to sort waste. Bags can be left in front of the door after letting the host know, and the host takes care of the rest.

sections.title: Restaurants & useful shops
sections.text: The Experiences & surroundings page brings together restaurants, walks and outing ideas around Libin. The quick buttons in this guide take you there directly.

points.title: Restaurants
points.text: Find favourite restaurants around Libin, with distance, cuisine style and directions when available.

points.title: Walks
points.text: Recommended walks include duration, distance and level when those details are available.

points.title: Useful shops
points.text: For a practical shop or a precise address, contact us and we will point you to the easiest option for your stay.

sections.title: Departure & check-out
sections.text: The idea is not to turn the end of the stay into a chore. A few simple gestures are enough.
bullets: Check that nothing is left behind in the cottage
Leave the kitchen in a reasonably clean condition
Sort the waste and leave it ready if needed
Close windows and switch off anything unnecessary
Respect the check-out time before 10:00 am

guide.faqDescription: A reassuring FAQ designed to reduce repetitive messages without sounding rigid.

faq.question: Can we arrive late?
faq.answer: Check-in is planned between 4 pm and 9 pm. If you expect to arrive towards the end of that window, it is best to let the host know.

faq.question: Is parking easy?
faq.answer: Yes. There are 7 parking spaces in total, so arrival is straightforward.

faq.question: Do we need to bring sheets or towels?
faq.answer: No. Everything is provided and beds are ready before arrival.

faq.question: Can we adjust the heating ourselves?
faq.answer: The heat pump is already set for comfort. If anything feels unusual, it is best to contact the host before changing the settings.

faq.question: How should we handle waste?
faq.answer: Guests are asked to sort waste. Bags can be left in front of the door after informing the host, who then handles the rest.

faq.question: Is someone easy to reach if there is a problem?
faq.answer: Yes. The host remains available, and the attached house allows quick support if needed.

practicalCards.title: Check-in / check-out
practicalCards.text: Arrival between 4 pm and 9 pm, departure before 10:00 am.

practicalCards.title: Bookings
practicalCards.text: The Booking page guides you between WhatsApp, the complete form and Airbnb.

form.title: Send an enquiry
form.text: For a general question, a practical detail or a special touch, simply leave us your message.

labels.subject: Subject
labels.consent: I agree to be contacted about my request.
labels.success: Your request has been sent. We will reply as soon as possible.
labels.invalid: Please check the required fields and your email address.
labels.error: Something went wrong while sending your message. You can contact us by e-mail or use WhatsApp.
labels.responsePreference: Reply by WhatsApp, e-mail or phone according to your preference.
labels.privacy: Your information is only used to answer your request.
